title: 'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhead: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.

title: Departure of Ukraine's popular defense minister sparks protests
Morocco's frenzy for purchasing surveillance tools revealed by whistleblower
Pegasus Project: Five years after revelations about a global phone surveillance system, judicial investigation continues
subhead: Two former executives of Israeli company NSO Group, which developed the spyware, were questioned in France. While the Elysée Palace promised in 2021 to 'fully shed light' on the surveillance of six government ministers, Paris has since forged closer diplomatic ties with Morocco, despite accusations that Rabat orchestrated the spying.

title: Assisted dying: French MPs moved by approval of 'historic' bill
subhead: Supporters and opponents of the bill debated it one last time on Wednesday before casting votes that saw it approved by a narrow margin. The government took a notably hands-off approach, and Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said he would refer three points of the new law to the Constitutional Council for review.

title: Tour de France: Tim Merlier claims third stage win
subhead: The Belgian rider claimed victory again in a crash-marred sprint in stage 12.

title: As her deputy questions promise to ban headscarves, Le Pen doubles down
subhead: Jordan Bardella has expressed doubts about one of the Rassemblement National's key proposals. But Marine Le Pen is considering putting it to a referendum, if she is elected.

subhead: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

title: For Macron, the legalization of aid in dying meant a long personal journey
subhead: After doubting the need for new legislation, fearing he might upset religious leaders and concerned about dividing French society, the president eventually resolved to support the legislation, under pressure from MPs and public opinion.

title: Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
subhead: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.
byline: Audrey Garric and  Raphaëlle Besse Desmoulières
